question: A box plot has minimum 10, Q1 = 15, median = 20, Q3 = 30, maximum = 40. What is the IQR?
A box plot has minimum 5, Q1 = 12, median = 18, Q3 = 25, maximum = 35. What is the range?
Data set: 3, 5, 7, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 20. Identify the five-number summary (min, Q1, median, Q3, max).
hints: Median is the middle value. Q1 is median of lower half. Q3 is median of upper half.

question: A box plot shows the middle 50% of data between 20 and 50. About what percent of the data falls above 50?
hints: The box contains the middle 50% (Q1 to Q3).
25% falls below Q1 and 25% falls above Q3.
solution: Q3 = 50. 25% of the data falls above Q3.

question: Two box plots compare test scores. Class A: min 55, Q1 65, median 75, Q3 85, max 95. Class B: min 60, Q1 70, median 78, Q3 82, max 90. Which class has more consistent scores, and how can you tell?
answer: Class B, because it has a smaller IQR (12 vs 20)
hints: Compare the IQR (spread of the middle 50%) for each class.
Class A IQR = 85−65 = 20. Class B IQR = 82−70 = 12.
solution: Class A IQR = 20. Class B IQR = 12. Class B is more consistent because its middle 50% of scores is more tightly packed.
